ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANTS PARAMOUNT GROUP, INC.'S AND 50 BEALE, INC.'S NOTICE OF DEMURRER AND DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT. HEARING HELD. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED AS MODIFIED: DEFENDANTS PARAMOUNT GROUP, INC.'S AND 50 BEALE INC.'S GENERAL DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED ON THE GROUND THAT THE COMPLAINT DOES NOT STATE FACTS SUFFICIENT TO CONSTITUTE A CAUSE OF ACTION AND BECAUSE, SUBSEQUENT TO THIS COURT'S RULING ON D. ZELINSKY & SONS, INC.'S DEMURRER, PLAINTIFFS HAVE REPRESENTED THAT THEY PLAN TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT TO ADDRESS THE ARGUMENTS MADE BY DEFENDANTS HERE. IT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO GIVE PLAINTIFFS AN OPPORTUNITY, IF THEY ARE ABLE TO DO SO, TO ALLEGE FACTS SUFFICIENT (1) TO MEET THE REQUIREMENTS OF BOCKRATH V. ALDRICH CHEMICAL CO., INC. (1999) 21 CAL.4TH 71 BY IDENTIFYING THE PRODUCT(S) FOR WHICH DEFENDANTS ARE ALLEGEDLY RESPONSIBLE, AND (2) TO IDENTIFY THE PREMISE(S) AT ISSUE FOR WHICH PLAINTIFFS ALLEGE DEFENDANTS, IDENTIFIED AS PREMISES DEFENDANTS, ARE RESPONSIBLE. PLAINTIFFS' AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DUE BY JULY 21, 2015. MS. SMITH IS TO PREPARE THE FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, NOT REPORTED. (503)

RULING:
DEFENDANT FOSTER WHEELER ENERGY CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Foster Wheeler Energy Corporation's motion for summary judgment is granted. Plaintiffs named defendant individually and as successor-in-interest to Barsotti's, Inc. As to plaintiffs' successor liability claims against defendant, defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to present evidence to create a triable issue whether defendant is liable for the liabilities of Barsotti's, Inc. As to plaintiffs' claims against defendant individually, plaintiffs concede that their sole claim against defendant is based on alleged exposure to asbestos attributable to Barsotti's, Inc.; plaintiffs make clear they do not allege that Mrs. Keays was exposed to a Foster Wheeler product. DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-24-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Deborah A. Smith of GORDON & REES LLP (510-463-8600), appearing for defendants / moving parties Frederick Spencer

RULING:
DEFENDANTS 50 BEALE INC. AND PARAMOUNT GROUP, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendants Paramount Group, Inc.'s and 50 Beale Inc.'s demurrer to plaintiffs' second amended complaint ("SAC") is sustained in part and overruled in part. Defendants' special demurrer to the First (negligence) cause of action is overruled. The SAC states facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action for negligence and is not uncertain. This Court already made this ruling on defendants' demurrer to plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint, after which plaintiffs did not have leave to amend the negligence cause of action. Defendants' special demurrer to the Second (negligence per se) cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. The SAC fails to state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action for negligence per se. Further, plaintiffs have already had an opportunity to amend this cause of action, did not attempt to demonstrate how this cause of action is properly alleged, did not request leave to amend and did not demonstrate a reasonable possibility that they can state a proper cause of action. Defendants' special demurrer to the Third (strict liability) is overruled as moot. Plaintiffs state that they have agreed to dismiss this cause of action against defendants. However, the Court does not see a dismissal on file. If a dismissal is not filed within 10 days, defendants may submit correspondence to the Court, copying plaintiffs' counsel, notifying the Court of this and submitting an updated proposed order that sustains the special demurrer to this cause of action without leave to amend. Defendants have 10 days to answer. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-22-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Ian P. Dillon of WFBM, LLP (415-781-7072), appearing for defendant / moving party D. Zelinsky and Sons, Inc.
Jonah D. King of SIMON GREENSTONE PANATIER BARTLETT, P.C. (562-590-3400), appearing via CourtCall for plaintiffs / opposing parties Beatrice Keays and Robert J. Keays

RULING:
DEFENDANT D. ZELINSKY AND SONS, INC.'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as amended: Defendant D. Zelinsky & Sons, Inc.'s demurrer to plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint ("FAC") is sustained in part and overruled in part. Defendant's general demurrer is overruled as plaintiff's FAC pleads facts sufficient to meet the requirements of Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical Co., Inc. (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71. Specifically, plaintiffs complied with this Court's prior order granting them leave to amend to identify the product(s) for which defendants are allegedly responsible. The FAC is not so uncertain that defendant cannot reasonably respond. Defendant's special demurrers to the First (negligence) and Fifth (loss of consortium) causes of action are overruled. The FAC states facts sufficient to constitute causes of action for negligence and loss of consortium and are not uncertain. Defendant's special demurrer to the Third (strict liability) cause of action is sustained without leave to amend for failure to state facts sufficient to constitute that cause of action. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Tara L. Williams of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13428 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

Defendant Paramount Group, Inc. And 50 Beal Inc.S Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Sep-17-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Deborah A. Smith of GORDON & REES LLP (510-463-8600), appearing for defendants / moving parties Paramount Group, Inc.'s and 50 Beale Inc.
Nectaria Belantis of SIMON GREENSTONE PANATIER BARTLETT, P.C. (562-590-3400), appearing via CourtCall for plaintiffs / opposing parties Beatrice Keays and Robert J. Keays.

RULING:
DEFENDANTS 50 BEALE INC. AND PARAMOUNT GROUP, INC.'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT

Hearing held. The Court made the following ruling: Defendants Paramount Group, Inc.'s and 50 Beale Inc.'s demurrer to plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint is overruled in part and sustained in part. The general demurrer is overruled. Defendants' special demurrers to the First (Negligence), Fourth (Premise defendants) and Fifth (Loss of Consortium) causes of action are overruled. Defendants' special demurrers to the Second (Negligence Per Se) and Third (Strict Liability) causes of action are sustained. Plaintiffs have 10 days leave to amend to state facts sufficient to constitute causes of action for negligence per se and strict liability. As to the negligence per se cause of action, plaintiffs have leave to amend to allege facts, if they are able to do so, to show that (1) defendants violated a statute, ordinance or regulation of a public entity, (2) the violation proximately caused plaintiff's injury, (3) the injury resulted from an occurrence of the nature which the statute was designed to prevent; (4) plaintiff was one of the class of persons for whose protection the statute was adopted. As to the strict liability cause of action, plaintiffs have leave to amend to allege facts, if they are able to do so, to show that defendants manufactured, supplied, distributed, placed into the stream of commerce or were in any way in the chain of distribution of their asbestos-containing products or could be liable under a strict liability theory in any other manner. Counsel for Plaintiffs is to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: David A. Disbrow, CSR #7768 (Tel: 415-298-1539) (503/TLJ)

Deborah A. Smith of GORDON & REES LLP, appearing for defendants /moving parties 50 Beale Inc. and Paramount Group, Inc. (510.463.8692), appearing for defendants/moving parties.

Jonah King of SIMON GREENSTONE PANATIER BARTLETT, P.C. (562. 590.3400), appearing by Court Call for plaintiffs/opposing parties Beatrice Keays and Robert Keays.

RULING:
DEFENDANTS PARAMOUNT GROUP, INC.'S AND 50 BEALE, INC.'S NOTICE OF DEMURRER AND DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT.

Matter called, discussions held. Mr. King requested an additional 10 days to file the amended complaint. Ms. Smith raised no objection to the request and inquired whether or not this would include Zelinsky. The Court granted Mr. King's request noting that this would be as to all defendants. The Court then adopted the tentative ruling as modified: Defendants Paramount Group, Inc.'s and 50 Beale Inc.'s general demurrer to plaintiffs' complaint is sustained on the ground that the complaint does not state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action and because, subsequent to this Court's ruling on D. Zelinsky & Sons, Inc.'s demurrer, plaintiffs have represented that they plan to amend the complaint to address the arguments made by defendants here. It is sustained with leave to amend to give plaintiffs an opportunity, if they are able to do so, to allege facts sufficient (1) to meet the requirements of Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical Co., Inc. (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71 by identifying the product(s) for which defendants are allegedly responsible, and (2) to identify the premise(s) at issue for which plaintiffs allege defendants, identified as Premises Defendants, are responsible. Plaintiffs' amended complaint is due by July 20, 2015. Ms. Smith is to prepare the form of order. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Not Reported. (503/TLJ)
